result:
Fixed the core problem: the site was mid-pivot from "InboxZap" (email cleanup tool) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), and the code hadn't caught up — which was actively suppressing organic traffic. Changes made in the workspace:

1. **Metadata mismatch** (`app/layout.tsx`) — title/description/OG tags said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes" while the actual page content is about Lumina's meeting-notes product. Rewrote to accurately describe Lumina with real target keywords, and added `metadataBase` for correct absolute URL resolution.
2. **Broken primary nav/CTA** — `/features` and `/pricing` were 404 (linked from nav and the homepage's main "Start free" CTA, but never built). Created both as real server-rendered pages.
3. **Dead redirect** — `/faq` permanently redirected to `/#faq`, but no such element existed on the homepage. Added a real FAQ section with that id.
4. **Blog was completely broken** — the index page was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist (always errored), and there were no `/blog/[slug]` routes at all despite GSC showing thousands of historical impressions on URLs like `/blog/post-1`–`/blog/post-12` (now full 404s — this was likely the single biggest source of lost traffic). Rebuilt the blog as server-rendered: a static content source (`lib/posts.ts`) with 12 posts on topics that actually match the current product (meeting notes, transcripts, action items) instead of the old email-cleanup topics, plus real `/blog/[slug]` pages with per-post metadata.
5. **Sitemap** — `sitemap.ts` had a TODO stubbing out blog posts entirely. Now includes all 12 post URLs plus `/features` and `/pricing`.
6. **Missing OG image** — referenced `/img/og-inboxzap.png`, which didn't exist (404 on every social share). Replaced with a dynamically generated `opengraph-image.tsx` via Next's `ImageResponse`.

Verified with a real `next build` (all pages statically prerendered, zero errors) and by curling every route on a locally-run dev server — `/`, `/features`, `/pricing`, `/faq`, `/blog`, all 12 post pages, `/sitemap.xml`, and the OG image all return correct status codes and content now.

One open strategic call I made: the old GSC-ranked blog topics (bulk-delete emails, Gmail storage, unsubscribe tools) are irrelevant to what Lumina sells today, so rather than resurrect that content verbatim I reused the same URLs with content aligned to the current product — preserves whatever URL equity remains without bait-and-switching visitors onto an unrelated tool.
